# Synthesized Constraints
<!-- generated by gsd-doc-synthesizer — do not hand-edit -->

---

## CONSTRAINT-001: ExtractionResult data structure contract

source: C:/RAG_workbench/docs/specs/eval-layer-spec.md
type: schema

The canonical output schema for the reader/eval pipeline is defined by these Python dataclasses:

- `Provenance` enum: XBRL | STRUCTURED_TABLE | NARRATIVE_LLM
- `ReasonCode` enum: OK | MISSING_FIELD | BAD_TYPE | OUT_OF_RANGE | IDENTITY_VIOLATION | XBRL_MISMATCH | UNKNOWN_CONCEPT | REFERENTIAL | NOVEL_FORM
- `Route` enum: AUTO | SAMPLED_REVIEW | ESCALATE
- `ExtractedField`: name, value, provenance, concept (optional), source_span (optional)
- `ExtractionResult`: cik, accession, form_type, period, fields (list[ExtractedField])
- `ValidationResult`: is_valid (bool), reason_codes (list[ReasonCode]), details (dict)
- `Decision`: route, confidence (float), validation (ValidationResult), triggers_fired (list[str])

All pipeline components must accept/emit these types. Stack assumed Python; if JS/TS translate interfaces before building.

---

## CONSTRAINT-002: Confidence scoring derivation rule

source: C:/RAG_workbench/docs/specs/eval-layer-spec.md
type: nfr

LLM self-reported confidence MUST NOT be used as a routing signal. Confidence MUST be derived from provenance + XBRL cross-validation:

- XBRL provenance base = 0.98
- STRUCTURED_TABLE provenance base = 0.85
- NARRATIVE_LLM provenance base = 0.55
- If XBRL tagged fact exists for the concept: confidence = 1.0 (match) or 0.0 (mismatch → ESCALATE)
- Record-level confidence = min (or weighted aggregate) over all fields

---

## CONSTRAINT-003: Routing band thresholds

source: C:/RAG_workbench/docs/specs/eval-layer-spec.md
type: nfr

Three routing tiers based on record-level confidence bands. Exact cut points MUST be calibrated from shadow deployment data (§6), not set by intuition. Until calibration is run, bands are undefined — do not hard-code values.

- HIGH → AUTO (act + log)
- MEDIUM → SAMPLED_REVIEW (act + queue a sample for human review)
- LOW or any always-escalate trigger → ESCALATE (hold for human)

---

## CONSTRAINT-004: Always-escalate trigger list (deterministic, non-bypassable)

source: C:/RAG_workbench/docs/specs/eval-layer-spec.md
type: protocol

The following conditions MUST trigger ESCALATE regardless of confidence score:

1. Balance sheet fails accounting identity check (after unit normalization)
2. Amended filing (/A suffix) or any restatement signal
3. 8-K material items 1.03 (bankruptcy), 4.02 (non-reliance/restatement), 4.01 (auditor change)
4. Any going-concern language detected
5. XBRL fact != text extraction for the same concept (XBRL_MISMATCH reason code)
6. Unrecognized us-gaap concept or new taxonomy version (UNKNOWN_CONCEPT, NOVEL_FORM)
7. Value outside historical range for that company
8. Any extraction feeding a user-facing financial figure, alert, or downstream action

Implemented as pure predicate functions over ExtractionResult; fired trigger names collected in Decision.triggers_fired.

---

## CONSTRAINT-005: SEC API access rules

source: C:/RAG_workbench/docs/specs/eval-layer-spec.md
type: protocol

All calls to SEC data APIs (companyfacts, bulk XBRL data sets) MUST:

- Declare a User-Agent header per SEC fair-access rules
- Respect rate limit: <= 10 requests per second

---

## CONSTRAINT-006: Validation structure — two mandatory layers

source: C:/RAG_workbench/docs/specs/eval-layer-spec.md
type: schema

Validation MUST produce a ValidationResult with both layers evaluated:

Schema layer checks:
- Required fields present per form type (10-K != 8-K field sets)
- Type correctness (dates parse, monetary values numeric, CIK = 10 digits, accession matches \d{10}-\d{2}-\d{6})
- Units sane (XBRL scale — thousands vs. actuals must not be confused)

Semantic layer checks:
- Accounting identities within tolerance: Assets ≈ Liabilities + StockholdersEquity; GrossProfit ≈ Revenues − CostOfRevenue; cash-flow subtotals tie
- Referential integrity: CIK ↔ company name; period dates consistent with fiscal period; referenced exhibits exist
- Plausibility vs. company history: pull prior values from companyfacts API, flag values > N std devs or > X% YoY from company-specific distribution → OUT_OF_RANGE

---

## CONSTRAINT-007: Human-agreement rate acceptance bar

source: C:/RAG_workbench/docs/specs/eval-layer-spec.md
type: nfr

Target human-agreement rate on the AUTO tier: > 95% before trusting the AUTO tier in production. Below this threshold, tighten confidence cut points or fix the extraction agent. Do not promote AUTO tier to production use without meeting this bar.

---

## CONSTRAINT-008: Drift alarm triggers

source: C:/RAG_workbench/docs/specs/eval-layer-spec.md
type: nfr

Drift monitoring MUST use human-agreement rate as the primary alarm signal. Escalation-rate movement is a secondary, ambiguous signal and MUST NOT be used as a sole alert trigger.

Alert conditions:
- Agreement rate drops below defined floor
- Unrecognized-concept count spikes (indicates new us-gaap taxonomy season)

Drift monitoring inputs: rolling agreement rate, input distribution (form types, sections, sizes, sectors), unrecognized-concept count.

---

## CONSTRAINT-009: Reader/extraction layer — use EdgarTools, do not write a parser

source: C:/RAG_workbench/docs/specs/eval-layer-spec.md
type: api-contract

The reader/extraction layer MUST be built on EdgarTools (dgunning/edgartools, MIT). Writing a custom EDGAR/XBRL parser is explicitly out of scope. EdgarTools already provides XBRL facts as DataFrames — this is also the ground-truth source for cross-validation.

Acceptable alternatives noted: edgar-crawler (lefterisloukas/edgar-crawler) for 10-K/10-Q/8-K item section JSON extraction.

---

## CONSTRAINT-010: Ground truth sourcing strategy

source: C:/RAG_workbench/docs/specs/eval-layer-spec.md
type: nfr

Ground truth MUST be sourced as follows:

- XBRL-tagged financial figures: use companyfacts API (live) and SEC Financial Statement Data Sets (bulk quarterly) — these are free automatic labels, do NOT hand-label
- Narrative-extracted fields with no XBRL equivalent (risk factors, 8-K events, qualitative items): hand-label 50–100 edge-case records
- Hand-labeling effort scoped only to the hard/narrative cases

---

## CONSTRAINT-011: Build order constraint (phases must not be reordered)

source: C:/RAG_workbench/docs/specs/eval-layer-spec.md
type: protocol

Mandated build sequence:

1. Data structures + reader adapter (wrap current output into ExtractionResult)
2. Schema validator (layer 1 of §4.1)
3. XBRL cross-validation + companyfacts client (unlocks semantic validation AND confidence scoring)
4. Semantic validator (layer 2 of §4.1)
5. Confidence + routing + triggers (§4.2–4.3)
6. Shadow deployment: run over historical filings without acting; calibrate AUTO/SAMPLED/ESCALATE cut points
7. Metrics dashboard (§4.4)
8. Review queue + feedback loop + drift alerts (§4.5)

Phases 1–6 must be complete before the system is considered trustworthy for read-only use. Phases 7–8 are required for sustained trustworthiness over time.
